channel.title: The Hindu: Latest News today from India and the World, Breaking news, Top Headlines and Trending News Videos.
channel.description: The Hindu Newspaper: Get latest News on Politics, Sports, Business, Arts, Entertainment and trending news Videos from The Hindu

image.title: The Hindu: Latest News today from India and the World, Breaking news, Top Headlines and Trending News Videos.

item.title: First-ever life-size Vivekananda statue in U.S. unveiled in Seattle
item.description: The unveiling of the statue was held on the occasion of the celebration of ICCR Day and is part of India’s broader cultural diplomacy initiatives

item.title: Unions, experts blame delay in Aavin milk supply on poor man-management
Asha Bhosle, legendary playback singer, passes away at 92 in Mumbai
item.description: Ms. Bhosle was admitted to a private hospital in Mumbai’s Breach Candy on Saturday (April 11) evening following cardiac arrest

item.title: Amarnath Yatra to begin from July 3; registrations to open from April 15
item.description: Pilgrims between the ages of 13 and 70 can undertake the yatra and registrations will begin from April 15, the L-G told reporters at the Lok Bhavan in Jammu

item.title: Chennai Poll Canvas: Harbour | Seeking growth, peace and stability
item.description: Chennai Poll Canvas is an election special programme from The Hindu, offering a comprehensive constituency roundup from in and around Chennai for the Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections 2026.

item.title: NTK’s transgender candidate vows to make Villivakkam a model constituency
item.description: She says the prevention of drug abuse among youngsters and ensuring women’s welfare will be her first priority if elected; there are plans to create playgrounds and fine arts centres to revive vanishing art forms; both the Dravidian majors have neglected issues for several decades, she adds

item.title: ‘Devious intentions’: Derek O'Brien attacks govt ahead of Parliament sitting on women's quota, delimitation
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday, April 12, wrote to floor leaders of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha, urging them to support the amendments to the women's reservation law during the special three-day session of Parliament.

item.title: Transgender Amendment Act: In their voice
item.description: As a new law tightens its grip on how trans identities are defined, an independent queer film cuts through the noise

item.title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: PM Modi to address poll rally in Siliguri today
item.description: Over 3.6 crore voters eligible for the first phase of Assembly polls in West Bengal, says the Election Commission of India

item.title: Election fever rises as political merchandise sales peak
item.description: The merchandise is priced upwards of ₹20, depending on size and material; most shops maintain social media accounts to advertise their latest additions

item.title: All the light we cannot see: Tamil Nadu’s match industry faces threat from cheap Chinese lighters
item.description: The match industry in Tamil Nadu, over a 100 years old, might have just met its most formidable enemy. A new threat has emerged in the form of cheap, imported, limited-use plastic lighters, and it looks like they may take the puff out of the safety match industry. S. Sundar reports on this phenomenon from Sivakasi, the match heartland of the South.

item.title: Hippo ‘Hamsini’ that attacked a veterinarian is in critical condition in Shivamogga Zoo
item.description: Wildlife veterinarians treating the animal suspect the origin of the infection could be the intrauterine foetal death

item.title: Sabrina Carpenter apologises after misidentifying cultural chant during Coachella set
item.description: Sabrina Carpenter has apologised after mistaking a traditional Arabic celebratory chant for yodeling during her Coachella set, following backlash over the onstage exchange

item.title: China says it will resume some ties with Taiwan including more direct flights
item.description: The Taiwan Work Office under China’s Communist Party issued a statement saying it would explore setting up a longstanding communication mechanism between the Communist Party and Taiwan’s Kuomingtang Party, and facilitate the import of Taiwan’s aquaculture products, after it banned them in recent years

item.title: From myths to language: examining the renaming of Kerala
item.description: The Union Government’s recent approval of the proposal to rename Kerala as Keralam prompts a re-examination of the term’s historicity, including its founding legend, the context of the United Kerala Movement, and arguments for the linguistic purity of ‘Keralam’

item.title: Aviation sector will need 35,000 pilots, 34,000 technicians in 10 years, says expert
Telangana Inter 2026 results announced; 70.58% candidates clear exam
item.description: The results of the first-year candidates, combining all streams, stood at 66.20%. In all, over 9.97 lakh candidates registered for the examination

item.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE updates: Iran says talks ended without deal due to 'excessive demands' made by U.S.
item.description: Vance departs Pakistan after failing to reach deal with Iran

item.title: What is the SIR controversy in West Bengal | Explained
item.description: How many names have been deleted from the electoral rolls, and why? What are the logical discrepancies identified by the ECI? Why were large numbers of voters placed under adjudication, and how was this resolved? How has the SIR process reshaped the State’s political contest?

item.title: Pakistan to continue facilitating U.S.-Iran talks, says Ishaq Dar; urges ceasefire
item.description: The Pakistan-brokered talks, the first direct, high-level engagement between the two sides since the 1979 Islamic Revolution, were watched globally amid increasing expectations of a breakthrough.

item.title: Nellore police arrest man who duped youth on pretext of providing jobs in TTD
item.description: Based on the complaint given by the duped victims, a case was registered at the Vedayapalem Police Station under the relevant sections of cheating and forgery; during investigation, ₹25.20 lakh was seized from the accused so far.

item.title: RSS-BJP's main objective is to finish off Constitution: Rahul Gandhi
item.description: "Ambedkar ji's main message was that of the Constitution. Without the Constitution, what we call India would not have been there,” said Mr. Gandhi.

item.title: Vance leaves Pakistan after Iran talks fail to reach a deal
item.description: No deal yet in US-Iran talks. Vice-President J.D. Vance says the U.S. is returning without an agreement after key differences over Iran’s nuclear programme. Iranian media says talks may continue, with the Strait of Hormuz at the centre of discussions.

item.title: PM Modi urges all political parties to clear amendments to Women’s Reservation Bill in ‘one voice’
item.description: In response to the Prime Minister’s letter, Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge questioned the timing of bringing in the amendments and pointed to the lack of consultations on legislation that would deeply affect the federal structure

item.title: Talks ended without deal due to 'excessive demands' made by U.S.: Iran
item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei, however, said Iran is determined to utilise all tools, including diplomacy, to secure national interests and protect the country's well-being

item.title: Dindigul Assembly constituency set to witness an intense battle
Tamil Nadu Assembly election: DMK announces seats for Secular Progressive Alliance
item.description: Seven allies to contest on the ‘Rising Sun’ symbol

item.title: We have created a safe environment that encourages women to speak up: DMK Minister Geetha Jeevan
item.description: Tamil Nadu Social Welfare Minister P. Geetha Jeevan says number of cases registered, chargesheets filed, and convictions secured have increased; highlighting steps taken to ensure fresh eggs for noon day meals, she promises efforts to ease traffic woes, boost healthcare and create more jobs in Thoothukudi

item.title: Punjab Government nod to amended sacrilege law with stricter punishments
item.description: The Cabinet approved the Jaagat Jot Sri Guru Granth Sahib Satkar (Amendment) Bill, 2026, which proposes harsher penalties, including life imprisonment, to deter sacrilege and protect the sanctity of Guru Granth Sahib

item.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: What is the share of seats for parties in AIADMK-led alliance?
item.description: Of the 234 Assembly constituencies in the State, the AIADMK will contest in 167 constituencies, while the remaining seats have been allotted to its alliance partners

item.title: Kerala Assembly polls: International delegates get a feel of election process in India
item.description: Seventeen delegates from Albania, Estonia, Fiji, Georgia, Kenya, Namibia, Samoa, Seychelles and Sierra Leone visit polling stations under the International Election Visitors’ Programme of the EC

item.title: ED files chargesheet against five in West Bengal ‘illegal’ coal syndicate case
item.description: The accused were also involved in the use of forged documents and extortion

item.title: Sri Lankan authorities seal state-run coal procurement firm's headquarters amid alleged irregularities
item.description: An audit report on the imports had cited irregularities in procedures, such as changing tender deadlines and alleged favour to certain suppliers

item.title: Life lessons from Hogwarts: Celebrating the Harry Potter movies
item.description: On this day in 2002, the first ever Harry Potter film ‘Harry Potter and the Sorcerer’s Stone’ hit theatres across India, contributing to an already growing fan base of witches and wizards. As we reflect on this historical day, let us recall some of the things we learnt from Harry and his friends.

item.title: Opposition raising law and order issues to divert attention: Vaiko
Delhi High Court rejects bail in minor rape case despite subsequent marriage
item.description: Justice Girish Kathpalia said that the marriage was performed by the accused simply as a ploy to get himself bailed out

item.title: A ‘developing’ community story with subplots in Chennai
item.description: A civic engagement of a kind that involves multiple stakeholders, Project Thiruvanmiyur Mada Street is refreshing its initiative to reclaim a public space once again

item.title: Prototype fast breeder reactor | Nuclear paradox
item.description: A milestone in India’s nuclear ambitions, but one shadowed by limited oversight and persistent concerns over transparency and accountability

item.title: Anna Nagar voters bring up unfinished business
item.description: Residents of localities in the Anna Nagar Assembly Constituency want civic initiatives that started well but petered out to be revived; post-elections, Federation of Anna Nagar Residents’ Associations is expected to meet up with the one who assumes office and present their views on how to make these exercises work for residents all over again

item.title: Islamabad talks: U.S. and Iran fail to reach agreement after 21 hours, Vance departs Pakistan
item.description: Vance also praised Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir, terming them “incredible hosts”

item.title: News in Frames: Traditions in transition
item.description: With a belief system intertwined with nature, and with music and dance intrinsic to daily life, an endangered tribe in Odisha strives to keep its visual tradition intact

item.title: US-Iran talks end without deal; Vance says setback worse for Tehran
item.description: No breakthrough in the first direct US-Iran talks in over a decade. Vice-President J.D. Vance said the U.S. is leaving Islamabad without a deal, after failing to secure assurances from Iran on nuclear weapons. Talks may continue, with the Strait of Hormuz seen as a major sticking point.

item.title: Government hurrying women’s quota law implementation for political mileage: Kharge to PM
item.description: Congress leader Kharge’s letter came in response to a missive from the Prime Minister on the special sitting of Parliament for a discussion on the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam from April 16

item.title: Rahul Gandhi, other Congress leaders to campaign in T.N. soon
item.description: Campaign details of party chief Kharge, Rahul, and Priyanka will be released soon, says AICC in-charge for Tamil Nadu Girish Chodankar

item.title: Samrat Choudhary | Bihar’s saffron face
item.description: As Nitish Kumar is likely to resign this week, the Deputy Chief Minister is the frontrunner to be the BJP’s first Chief Minister of the State

item.title: Jaishankar meets UAE Deputy PM, discusses regional situation
item.description: Jaishankar is in Abu Dhabi in the concluding leg of his four-day two-nation visit to Mauritius and the UAE from April 9-12

item.title: Lack of funds won’t hamper growth of agriculture sector, says Rajnath Singh
item.description: Mr. Singh also said that the Defence Ministry has taken a decision under which, in the cantonment areas, vegetables and fruits are being bought only from the nearby farmers who practice organic farming

item.title: Olympic Committee’s ban on trans women in female category | Explained
item.description: The IOC has claimed the move would ensure a “level playing field” for female athletes, but this comes at a time when instances of discrimination against transgender people are rising globally, and critics point out that the test to be used is far from 100% accurate

item.title: Farmer dies in elephant attack in Karnataka’s Belur
item.description: Human-elephant conflicts have been a recurring problem for many years in parts of the Belur, Sakleshpur, and Alur taluks of Hassan District

item.title: Hormuz deal to shape resumption of Qatari LNG/LPG imports
item.description: The complex dynamics of who ends up controlling the strait will be key in resuming imports to India, or other countries, for that matter

item.title: Pakistan deploys 13,000 troops and fighter jets to Saudi Arabia
item.description: The deployment aimed to enhance joint military coordination, improve operational readiness between the armed forces of the two countries, and support security and stability at regional and international levels

item.title: CBSE’s measured step toward integrating Computational Thinking and AI in everyday learning
In Chennai, Crafts Council of India celebrates the sari and more, at their annual textile showcase
item.description: Tari — The Loom, the annual textile show of the Crafts Council of India will showcase the sari, and a range of textiles from 31 designers and weavers on April 10 and 11 in Chennai

item.title: Flood safety works for Bhogapuram international airport fast-tracked
item.description: ₹19-crore contract awarded to RKN Infrastructures to set up an advanced drainage and rainwater management system to shield the airport from natural hazards

item.title: Centre raises export duties on diesel, aviation turbine fuel
item.description: The Government hikes windfall tax on diesel to ₹55.5 per litre and ATF ₹42 per litre with immediate effect

item.title: From a jail, forest, and space
Two-day water sports competitions begin in Vijayawada
Anti-incumbency wave prevails in T.N., says Anbumani
item.description: PMK leader Anbumani Ramadoss campaigned for NDA candidates in the district

item.title: Iran and Strait of Hormuz: A lone battle for control
item.description: Historically, international straits have been flashpoints between littoral states and external powers, often serving as bargaining tools during conflicts

item.title: U.S., Iran talks extend into second day as strait showdown deepens
item.description: In a break with precedent, Iranian and U.S. officials were meeting directly, alongside Pakistan, and not speaking through mediators who shuttled between rooms
